mechanism_narrative: Sustained low unemployment indicates a strong labor market, supporting consumer spending and corporate profits, which translates to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:low unemployment generally correlates with economic growth
failure_condition: If UNRATE remains low (e.g., below 5%) but S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are consistently negative over a 1-year period, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is at a historically low level, it signals an accommodative monetary policy, encouraging investment and risk-taking, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:low interest rates typically support equity valuations
failure_condition: If FEDFUNDS remains below 0.25% for 6 consecutive months, but S&P 500 forward 60-day returns are negative more than 50% of the time during this period.

mechanism_narrative: Strong retail sales combined with robust employment growth indicate healthy consumer demand and income, driving corporate earnings and leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS growth > 0.5% MoM AND PAYEMS growth > 0.1% MoM
failure_condition: If both RSAFS and PAYEMS show strong growth (as per trigger) for 3 consecutive months, but S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are negative in more than 40% of those instances.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of industrial expansion, increasing industrial production signals strong business investment and manufacturing activity, which translates to positive S&P 500 forward returns, especially when overall demand (GDP) is also growing, over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: If INDPRO shows consistent growth during an expansionary period (GDPC1 growth > 1% annualized), but S&P 500 forward 60-day returns are flat or negative for 2 consecutive quarters.

mechanism_narrative: A significant decline in housing starts below a critical threshold indicates weakening consumer confidence and future economic activity, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:housing starts are a leading economic indicator
failure_condition: If HOUST drops below 500.0 for 3 consecutive months, but S&P 500 forward 60-day returns are positive in more than 50% of those periods.

mechanism_narrative: Persistently low inflation, as measured by CPI, allows central banks to maintain accommodative policies, supporting equity valuations and leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:low inflation generally supports higher P/E ratios
failure_condition: If CPIAUCSL annualized growth remains below 2% for 1 year, but S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are consistently negative (e.g., average return < 0).

mechanism_narrative: When unemployment is low but inflation is rising, it signals potential overheating and prompts central bank tightening, which negatively impacts equity markets and leads to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Phillips curve dynamics, central bank reaction function
failure_condition: If UNRATE drops below 5.0% and CPIAUCSL shows sustained monthly growth above 0.3% for 6 months, but S&P 500 forward 60-day returns are predominantly positive (e.g., > 60% positive).

mechanism_narrative: During periods of strong economic growth, as indicated by GDP, robust demand translates directly into higher corporate revenues and profits, driving S&P 500 forward returns positively over the next 5 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:GDP growth is a fundamental driver of equity markets
failure_condition: If GDPC1 growth exceeds 2.0% annualized for two consecutive quarters, but S&P 500 forward 5-day returns are negative in more than 55% of the observations during that period.
